text: When designing a REST API, it is important to follow best practices to make it easy for developers to use. First, create clear and logical endpoints for your resources. For example, use `/users` for user data and `/products` for product data. Using proper HTTP status codes is also essential—200 for success, 201 for creation, 400 for client errors, and 500 for server issues. Additionally, always provide good documentation that explains how to use each endpoint, including examples. Finally, include versioning in your API, like `/v1/users`, so that changes don't break existing users. Good design helps everyone work more efficiently.
